5148	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
5149	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the
5150	  affine cover system (Definition~\ref{def:affine_cover_system},
5151	  Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}): for every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) and every
5152	  \(p > 0\) one has \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This is the seed that supplies
5153	  condition (3) of the basis-comparison Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, and is the 01I8
5154	  globalisation of the tilde-case standard-cover vanishing.

5158	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} the quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is isomorphic to
5159	  \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\), with sections over each face \(D(g_\sigma)\)
5160	  the away localisation \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
5161	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} applies to the tilde sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\): its section
5162	  {\v C}ech complex over any standard cover is exact in positive degrees. Transporting along the
5163	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\), which is natural in the coefficient and so induces
5164	  an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech complexes, gives \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
5165	  for all \(p > 0\) and every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\). Hence \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing
5166	  higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the affine cover system.
5167	\end{proof}

5180	\subsection{Section {\v C}ech infrastructure and the cover-system encoding}
5181	
5182	The sub-lemmas below are formalized in a \emph{cover-local}, presheaf-level, hypothesis-driven
5183	form: each lemma fixes a single index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) and works with the
5184	section {\v C}ech complexes of that one covering, taking the geometric inputs (section-level
5185	exactness on faces, positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing) as hypotheses. The cover-global
5186	\((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) data of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} re-enters only
5187	in the inductive step (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}), where it is packaged by
5188	the cover-system encoding (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system},
5189	Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) and discharges those hypotheses via the per-face
5190	short-exact-sequence derivation (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}). The supporting
5191	definitions and the degreewise product engine are collected first.

5224	  on the basic-open section group of each intersection it is the map

5226	  i_p})\). These assemble into a cosimplicial morphism (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial
5227	  map}), functorial in \(\varphi\) (the \emph{section {\v C}ech cosimplicial functor}). Composing
5228	  with the alternating-coface-map complex functor packages the section {\v C}ech complex as a
5229	  functor in the coefficient presheaf (the \emph{section {\v C}ech complex functor}) and yields,
5230	  for each \(\varphi\), the induced chain map of section {\v C}ech complexes

5282	[Cover system on a basis]
5283	  \label{def:basis_cov_system}
5284	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.BasisCovSystem, AlgebraicGeometry.CovDatum}
5285	  \uses{def:cech_complex, lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:injective_cech_acyclic}
5286	  \textit{Project-bespoke encoding of the hypotheses of Stacks 01EO.}
5287	  A \emph{cover system on a basis} for a scheme \(X\) is a record with five fields. A
5288	  \emph{covering datum} (the helper \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\)) is a pair \((\iota, U)\) of an index type
5289	  \(\iota\) together with an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\); it is the raw shape
5290	  \(\Sigma\,\iota,\ \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) over which the section {\v C}ech complex is built.
5291	  The cover system bundles:
5292	  \begin{enumerate}
5293	  \item a basis \(\mathcal{B} \subseteq \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) for the topology;
5294	  \item a set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of admissible coverings, each a \(\mathrm{CovDatum}\);
5295	  \item \emph{faces-in-basis} (\(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\)): for every covering in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) every
5296	    finite intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) of its opens lies in \(\mathcal{B}\) (condition (1) of
5297	    Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis});
5298	  \item \emph{section surjectivity} (\(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\)): for every \(V \in \mathcal{B}\)
5299	    and every short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
5300	    whose left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive {\v C}ech cohomology over the coverings of \(V\) in
5301	    \(\mathrm{Cov}\), the section map \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective;
5302	  \item \emph{injective acyclicity} (\(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\)): for every injective
5303	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) and every covering \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) the
5304	    positive-degree {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes, \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for
5305	    all \(p > 0\).
5306	  \end{enumerate}
5307	  Unlike a bare combinatorial record, the structure carries two sheaf-theoretic ({\v C}ech-cohomology)
5308	  fields. The field \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) is \emph{not} the raw cofinality datum: it is the
5309	  section-surjectivity \emph{output} that cofinality together with Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}
5310	  produces, packaged directly as the consequence the 01EO chain consumes. The field
5311	  \(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\) is the injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity of
5312	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} for the system's own coverings. These are exactly the data
5313	  that the dimension-shift induction Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} consumes.
5314	\end{definition}

1	\chapter{Higher direct images $R^i f_*$ of quasi-coherent sheaves ($i \geq 1$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, definition of $R^i f_*$ as the
5	% $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex
6	% and references/stacks-coherent.md).
7	
8	\section{Setup and motivation}
9	
10	This chapter records the derived-functor higher direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\)
11	for \(i \geq 1\). It provides the single object that the {\v C}ech computation of
12	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage} compares against: the comparison
13	theorem \(\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}\) identifies the cohomology of the
14	relative {\v C}ech complex with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) wherever the derived functor
15	is defined.
16	
17	We use the classical cohomology-sheaf formulation (not the derived category).
18	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
19	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
20	
21	\section{Definition of the higher direct images}
22	
23	\begin{definition}\leanok
24	[Higher direct image]
25	  \label{def:higher_direct_image}
26	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage}
27	  % NOTE (iter-233): the Lean `higherDirectImage` carries an explicit instance
28	  % hypothesis `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`, because Mathlib has
29	  % `Abelian X.Modules` but NOT `EnoughInjectives` / `IsGrothendieckAbelian` for
30	  % `SheafOfModules` over a sheaf of rings (the Grothendieck-abelian instance is
31	  % only for `Sheaf J A` with a fixed value category). The definition is therefore
32	  % conditional; the hypothesis can be dropped once a lane proves that instance.
33	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
34	  % `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing' (proof), describing $R^i f_*$ as the
35	  % $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ via the sheafified presheaf
36	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$
37	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L188--192).
38	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "According to
39	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
40	  % the sheaf
41	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
42	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$."
43	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Quasi-coherence of
44	  higher direct images (and the underlying Cohomology chapter).}
45	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
46	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For \(i \geq 0\) the \emph{\(i\)-th
47	  higher direct image} \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(i\)-th right derived functor
48	  of the pushforward functor \(f_*\) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\). Concretely,
49	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module obtained as the
50	  sheafification of the presheaf
51	  \[
52	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^i\bigl(f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
53	    \qquad V \subset S \text{ open},
54	  \]
55	  where \(H^i(-, -)\) denotes sheaf cohomology. For \(i = 0\) this recovers the
56	  ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
57	\end{definition}

291	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
292	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)

298	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.counit) R.obj =

312	  counitIso :=

315	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ pushforwardId _
